data.title: Canadian Retailer Roots (ROOT) Is Exploring 'Strategic Alternatives' as Losses Pile Up
data.description:
Canadian clothing retailer Roots Corp. (TSE:$ROOT) says it is exploring "strategic alternatives" after reporting a first-quarter loss of C$10.1 million (US$7.23 million).

Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

  • Deliver institutional-grade market data directly into Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and other MCP-compatible AI tools.
  • Designed for personal research, portfolio monitoring, and AI-assisted investment workflows.

The Toronto-based company, known for its sweat pants and logo that depicts a beaver, had previously said that it is open to a possible sale of the company. However, no buyers have stepped forward to acquire Roots, whose financial situation is deteriorating.

For this year's first quarter, Roots reported a loss of C$0.26 per share compared with a loss of C$0.20 a year earlier. Sales in the quarter totaled C$42.6 million, up from $40 million a year earlier. The company has increased its marketing and offered discounts to attract consumers.

Bankruptcy Rumors

Management at Roots said the increase in revenue resulted largely from direct-to-consumer sales that totaled $35.8 million, up from $34.6 million a year earlier, while partner sales amounted to $6.8 million, up from $5.4 million a year ago.

In March of this year, Roots launched a strategic review of its operations in an effort to maximize value for shareholders. Now, the company says it is evaluating a range of alternatives for the business. Rumors and speculation online say that could mean a bankruptcy filing by Roots. The company reported a net loss of C$33.4 million in 2025. ROOT stock has declined 81% in the past decade.

Is ROOT Stock a Buy?

Not enough analysts cover Roots' stock, so instead we'll look at the year-to-date performance of its share price. As one can see in the chart below, ROOT stock has risen 49% this year after management said the company is for sale.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Ahead of Micron's (MU) Q3 Earnings, Let's Look at Who Owns the Memory Chip Stock
data.description:
Micron Technology (MU), a maker of memory and storage chips, is set to report results for the third quarter of fiscal 2026 on June 24. The stock has surged 246% do far this year as strong AI demand boosted sales of memory chips. Higher memory prices, helped by tight supply, have also supported the rally.

Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

  • Deliver institutional-grade market data directly into Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and other MCP-compatible AI tools.
  • Designed for personal research, portfolio monitoring, and AI-assisted investment workflows.

Wall Street expects Micron to report Q3 FY26 earnings per share (EPS) of $19.95, indicating a solid 942% year-over-year growth. Revenue is expected to rise 272% to $34.66 billion. The solid growth estimates reflect the strong demand for high-bandwidth memory (HBM) and DRAM solutions in data centers. Ahead of Q3 FY26 results, several analysts have raised their price targets for MU stock.

Now, according to TipRanks' Ownership Tool, exchange-traded funds (ETFs) own 29.96% of Micron. They are followed by public companies and individual investors, mutual funds, other institutional investors, and insiders at 29.36%, 22.96%, 17.51%, and 0.21%, respectively.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: RKLB, SPCE, LUNR: Space Stocks Fall Hard after SpaceX Blockbuster IPO Opens on Nasdaq
data.description:
Space stocks sank sharply on Friday as Space Exploration Technologies (SPCX), or SpaceX, made its long‑awaited public debut on the Nasdaq (NDAQ). Shares of Rocket Lab (RKLB), Virgin Galactic (SPCE), Intuitive Machines (LUNR), Firefly Aerospace (FLY), and EchoStar (SATS) all fell more than 10% at the open, giving back much of yesterday's big rally. Elon Musk and SpaceX President and COO Gwynne Shotwell rang the opening bell on Friday, marking one of the most anticipated IPO moments in years.

Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

Explore FLYT for 2X leverage on FLY

Hard Reversal After Yesterday's Surge

The sector had soared on Thursday as traders piled into anything tied to space ahead of the historic listing. But once SpaceX began trading, the sentiment shifted. Investors rushed to take profits, unwind speculative bets, and shift capital toward the SpaceX IPO itself.

Fund managers and retail traders are also selling other space names to free up cash for the massive offering. Many of these stocks had run up sharply over the past week, making them prime targets for "sell‑the‑news" moves now that the IPO is live.

Details of the SpaceX IPO

SpaceX's debut ranks among the biggest in market history, with the company raising $75 billion by selling 555.6 million shares at a fixed price of $135 each, giving it a valuation near $1.8 trillion. Investor demand was massive, with more than $250 billion in orders placed for the offering.

That means the deal was oversubscribed nearly four times, drawing intense interest from both big institutions and retail buyers. The size of the offering has led investors to reshuffle portfolios, adding pressure to other space stocks.

It is worth noting that trading does not start the moment the market opens. After the 9:30 a.m. bell, the stock went into an auction phase so buy and sell orders could line up and set a fair opening price. For a big IPO like this, steady trading can take a few hours to really start.

Is SPCX Stock a Good Buy?

Based on the rating of a single analyst covering SpaceX, Timothy Horan from Oppenheimer, the stock is a Moderate Buy. His price target of $190 implies about 41% upside from the IPO price of $135 per share.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: Oil Exports from Middle East to Normalize by Late August, Says Goldman Sachs (GS)
data.description:
Wall Street investment bank Goldman Sachs (GS) says that oil exports from the Middle East are likely to normalize by late August now that it appears the Strait of Hormuz will reopen to commercial ships.

Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

  • Deliver institutional-grade market data directly into Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and other MCP-compatible AI tools.
  • Designed for personal research, portfolio monitoring, and AI-assisted investment workflows.

Commodities analysts at Goldman Sachs also maintained their year-end forecast for Brent crude oil's price at $90 per barrel. The new outlook from the bank comes a day after U.S. President Donald Trump indicated that the U.S. and Iran have reached a peace deal that will see the Strait of Hormuz reopened within 30 days.

Goldman Sachs forecasts a five to six million barrels per day oil deficit in the current second quarter, but sees that deficit easing as commercial traffic resumes in the Strait of Hormuz, which is where about 20% of the world's crude oil is typically shipped.

Crude Oil Prices Fall

Crude oil prices have declined about 5% in the past 24 hours and dropped below $90 U.S. a barrel on reports that the U.S. and Iran have reached a deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. U.S. crude oil prices declined as much as 5% to $83.80 a barrel. Brent crude oil, the international benchmark, also fell 5% to $86.45 per barrel.

The peace deal reportedly includes a commitment from Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz within 30 days while the U.S. would lift oil sanctions on the country. Additionally, all U.S. military forces would retreat from Iran, and the U.S. and its allies will facilitate reconstruction of Iran at a cost of $300 billion.

Comparing Oil Stocks

Below is a chart comparing the stocks of oil majors Chevron (CVX), Shell (SHEL), and Occidental Petroleum (OXY). As one can see, all three stocks carry Buy ratings, although Chevron is the only one with a Strong Buy recommendation. Analysts continue to see upside potential in all three oil stocks.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: S&P 500 Rebounds as Consumer Sentiment Blasts Past Estimates
data.description:
The S&P 500 (SPX) staged a red-to-green comeback after the University of Michigan provided its latest consumer sentiment data.

Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

  • Deliver institutional-grade market data directly into Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and other MCP-compatible AI tools.
  • Designed for personal research, portfolio monitoring, and AI-assisted investment workflows.

The preliminary Index of Consumer Sentiment for June clocked in at 48.9, above expectations of 46. The index was at 44.8 in May, an all-time low. Falling gas prices were a key driver behind the rebound, lifting sentiment across age, education, and political groups.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Nvidia vs. AMD vs. Intel: What If the Bears Are Right About Chip Stocks?
data.description:
Chip stocks are starting to recover on Friday after the tech sector went through roughly a week of selloffs. This was triggered by Broadcom's (AVGO) latest earnings report. The report included AI semiconductor revenue guidance below Wall Street's estimates. This sparked concerns that the bears could be right and AI chip stocks are in a bubble that could pop anytime now.

Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

High conviction NVDA bears now have this Tradr ETF

What Would an AI Bubble Pop Do to Top Chip Stocks?

If the AI sector really is in a bubble, and that bubble pops, it could have extreme negative effects on the price of top chip stocks. Let's see what the most bearish analysts expect for AI chip leaders Nvidia (NVDA), Advanced Micro Devices (AMD), and Intel (INTC) below.

  • Five-star DBS analyst Fang Boon Foo holds the lowest price target for NVDA stock at $250. However, that still suggests a possible 21.52% upside for the shares. He also has a Buy rating, which was reiterated on June 5, 2026. Foo expects demand for Nvidia's Hopper and Blackwell to outpace supply into 2028.
  • Five-star Deutsche Bank analyst Ross Seymore holds the Street-low price target of $250 for AMD. This represents a potential 50.91% downside for the shares. He also has a Hold rating that was reiterated on April 28, 2026. Seymore said that AMD's EPYC server CPUs are expected to experience strong growth. Even so, the analyst sees supply issues as limiting the company's growth potential.
  • Five-star Wedbush analyst Matt Bryson holds the lowest price target for INTC stock at $30. This implies a possible 75.05% downside for the shares. He also has a Hold rating that was reiterated on April 20, 2026. Bryson argued that Intel is valued at 60 times his out-year estimates, and that he doesn't see "industry trends supporting such an aggressive valuation."

It's important to note that even the most bearish analysts covering major chip stocks don't appear to believe that AI is in a bubble. Taking that into account, the price of NVDA, AMD, and INTC shares could fall further than these targets if AI is in a bubble and it does pop.

NVDA vs. AMD vs. INTC: Where Do Analysts Stand on These Chip Stocks?

Turning to the TipRanks stock comparison tool, traders can see what the analysts' consensus and average price targets are for each of these semiconductor stocks. NVDA and AMD are both rated Strong Buy by analysts, while INTC is rated Hold. The only upside potential among them belongs to Nvidia at 52.12%. AMD stock has a downside potential of 5.56% followed by a possible 24.34% downside for Intel stock.
data.author: William White

data.title: Nasdaq 100 Rebalancing Welcomes Five New Stocks
data.description:
On Thursday evening, Nasdaq (NDAQ) announced the results of its June 2026 quarterly rebalance. The rebalance will be effective ahead of the market open on Monday, June 22. Here are the five stocks that made the cut:

Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

LABX: an alternative to margin or options on ALAB

  • Astera Labs (ALAB)
  • CoreWeave (CRWV)
  • Nebius (NBIS)
  • Rocket Lab (RKLB)
  • Teradyne (TER)

The Nasdaq 100 tracks 100 of the largest non-financial companies listed on the Nasdaq exchange. However, the index consists of over 100 stocks since Alphabet (GOOGL) has multiple share classes.

The Five Companies Leaving the Nasdaq 100

Only 100 companies are allowed in the index. Due to the new additions, five companies received the boot:

  • Charter Communications (CHTR)
  • Cognizant Technology (CTSH)
  • Insmed Incorporated (INSM)
  • Verisk Analytics (VRSK)
  • Zscaler (ZS)

To be eligible for inclusion, a company must have enough liquidity and public float while also ranking among the largest Nasdaq-listed companies by market capitalization.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Why Is Penny Stock Park Ha Biological Technology (BYAH) Up Today?
data.description:
Park Ha Biological Technology (BYAH) stock rocketed higher on Friday after the company announced a new strategic collaboration. The skincare solutions company's new partnership is with AI company Star Plus Action. The two companies intend to work together in the healthcare industry, skincare product development, celebrity IP management, and artificial intelligence technology. This is all with the goal of advancing the beauty and wellness sector.

Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

  • Deliver institutional-grade market data directly into Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and other MCP-compatible AI tools.
  • Designed for personal research, portfolio monitoring, and AI-assisted investment workflows.

Park Ha Biological Technology and Star Plus Action have already launched their first product together. This product is AI Nutritionist, which offers nutrition advice to users. The company also wants to release AI-powered store managers and AI-driven professional skincare specialists.

Park Ha Biological Technology's CEO said that the agreement "represents a pivotal upgrade for the brand, transitioning from a skincare-only focus to a comprehensive health and wellness ecosystem." The company intends to leverage its R&D, IP resources, and industrial expertise in combination with Star Plus Action's AI abilities to enhance product offerings and intelligent service systems. It argued this will allow it to create a differentiated core competitive advantage in the health and wellness industry.

Park Ha Biological Technology Stock Movement Today

Park Ha Biological Technology stock was up 153.33% in premarket trading on Friday, following a 15.32% fall yesterday. The stock has also decreased 86.79% year-to-date and 99.89% over the past 12 months.

Today's news brought heavy trading to BYAH stock, as some 47 million shares changed hands. For comparison, the company's three-month average daily trading volume is below 1 million shares.

Is Park Ha Biological Technology Stock a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

Turning to Wall Street, Park Ha Biological Technology stock lacks significant analyst coverage. This leaves traders without guidance when handling the stock. However, they should note the stock's recent market movement. While the stock was up today, its recent falls should act as a red flag for potential investors. It has also had trouble with delisting warnings.
data.author: William White

data.title: CoreWeave Stock Forecast – NDX Inclusion, Strong Growth, and Mixed Analyst Views
data.description:
Shares of cloud infrastructure provider CoreWeave (CRWV) were up 4.9% in pre-market trading, following news of its inclusion in the Nasdaq-100 Index (NDX). Nasdaq (NDAQ) announced that it is adding five companies, including CoreWeave, Nebius (NBIS), Rocket Lab (RKLB), Astera Labs (ALAB), and Teradyne (TER) to the NDX, effective June 22.

Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

LABX: an alternative to margin or options on ALAB

Inclusion in the Nasdaq-100 is often a positive catalyst for a company. Index funds and ETFs tracking the index must buy the stock. This boosts demand for the stock, trading volume, and the share price in the short term. The changes are being made as part of Nasdaq's June quarterly rebalance.

CoreWeave's Financials Are Improving

CRWV stock has gained 33.7% so far in 2026. The rally has been driven by surging demand for AI infrastructure and GPU capacity. In Q1FY26, CoreWeave's sales increased by 127% year-over-year, with revenue hitting $2.08 billion. CoreWeave also saw the strongest bookings quarter in its history, with revenue backlog reaching $99.4 billion as of March 31.

Moreover, the company signed major new agreements with Meta (META), including a $21 billion commitment, and reached a multi-year deal with Anthropic to support the development of the Claude AI model.

Analysts Remain Divided Despite the Strong Rally

Analysts remain cautiously optimistic about CoreWeave's long-term outlook. Recently, Cantor Fitzgerald analyst Brett Knoblauch maintained his Buy rating and $167 price target (74.4% upside potential). He noted that CoreWeave has gained about 23% since the end of March, trailing many AI infrastructure peers, and called it a buying opportunity. He highlighted that CoreWeave has a much larger capacity than competitors, an expanding software platform, and a strong position to benefit from future AI demand.

On the other hand, Bernstein analyst Madison Rezaei reiterated his Sell rating on the stock, with a $67 price target (30% downside potential). He remains cautious on CoreWeave's long-term business outlook but does not see recent insider sales as a meaningful near-term concern. However, significant selling by major shareholders such as Jane Street or Nvidia (NVDA) could change that view.

Is CRWV a Good Stock to Buy?

On TipRanks, CRWV has a Moderate Buy consensus rating based on 13 Buys, nine Holds, and one Sell rating. The average CoreWeave price target of $132.35 implies 38.2% upside potential from current levels.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Dow Jones Climbs ahead of Highly-Anticipated SpaceX IPO
data.description:
The Dow Jones (DJIA) is set to open Friday higher ahead of SpaceX's IPO at 9:50 Eastern Time. Shares of the spaceflight and telecommunications company are set to open at $135. That's equivalent to a $1.77 trillion valuation.

Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

  • Deliver institutional-grade market data directly into Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and other MCP-compatible AI tools.
  • Designed for personal research, portfolio monitoring, and AI-assisted investment workflows.

The IPO is expected to elevate Elon Musk's net worth to over $1 trillion. He currently owns over $270 billion in Tesla (TSLA) stock and options. The SpaceX IPO will likely provide with him another $841 billion.

Record SpaceX IPO Could Drive Market Volatility

SpaceX will raise a total of $75 billion by offering 555.6 million shares. 20% of the shares are allocated to retail investors. That's much higher than the typical 5% to 10%.

The IPO will be the largest of all time and could create volatility. This could occur due to investors selling out of other stocks to fund their SpaceX purchases. SpaceX is also considering bond sales, offering investors an alternative way to gain exposure without owning stock.
data.author: Eddie Pan
